text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient Name: Margaret Okonkwo
Medical Record Number: [REDACTED]
Accession Number: SP-[REDACTED]
Ordering Physician: [REDACTED], MD (Urology/Surgical Oncology)
Attending Pathologist: [REDACTED], MD
Resident/Fellow: [REDACTED], MD (PGY-4, Pathology)

CLINICAL HISTORY:
63-year-old female with stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC), right kidney, presenting with an 11.7 cm right renal mass with venous extension into the right renal vein, retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, and bilateral pulmonary metastases. Prior CT-guided core needle biopsy confirmed clear cell RCC, ISUP/WHO grade 3, with sarcomatoid differentiation. Patient underwent open right radical nephrectomy with regional retroperitoneal lymph node dissection for cytoreductive intent. Pre-operative staging: T3b N2 M1, AJCC 8th edition.

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
A. Right kidney, radical nephrectomy
B. Right para-aortic lymph nodes (3 lymph nodes submitted in separate container, labeled by surgeon)
C. Right aortocaval lymph nodes (2 lymph nodes submitted in separate container, labeled by surgeon)

---

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:

A. RIGHT KIDNEY, RADICAL NEPHRECTOMY:
   - CLEAR CELL RENAL CELL CARCINOMA (ccRCC)
     - ISUP/WHO Nuclear Grade: 4 (grade 4 nucleoli easily visible at 10x magnification; tumor giant cells present focally)
     - Tumor size: 11.7 cm in greatest dimension
     - Sarcomatoid differentiation: PRESENT, comprising approximately 20% of total tumor volume
     - Rhabdoid differentiation: ABSENT
     - Coagulative tumor necrosis: EXTENSIVE, involving greater than 50% of sampled tumor
     - Lymphovascular invasion: PRESENT (renal vein tumor thrombus, see below)
     - Perirenal fat invasion: PRESENT
     - Renal sinus fat invasion: PRESENT
     - Renal vein (main trunk): POSITIVE FOR TUMOR THROMBUS (macroscopic, involving main renal vein)
     - Inferior vena cava: NOT IDENTIFIED / NOT INVOLVED (clinically confirmed intraoperatively)
     - Ureter: Negative for tumor; no invasion
     - Adrenal gland: NOT SUBMITTED (not resected per operative note; clinically uninvolved)
     - Gerota's fascia: INTACT; surgical resection margin negative for carcinoma
     - Renal capsule: Invaded (tumor extends into perirenal fat but contained within Gerota's fascia)
     - PATHOLOGIC STAGE (AJCC 8th Edition): pT3b

B. RIGHT PARA-AORTIC LYMPH NODES:
   - 2 of 3 lymph nodes positive for metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma
   - Largest positive node: 3.0 cm in greatest dimension
   - Extranodal extension: PRESENT (focal, < 2 mm beyond lymph node capsule)

C. RIGHT AORTOCAVAL LYMPH NODES:
   - 1 of 2 lymph nodes positive for metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma
   - Largest positive node: 1.8 cm in greatest dimension
   - Extranodal extension: ABSENT

OVERALL PATHOLOGIC STAGING (AJCC 8th Edition, Kidney):
   pT3b pN2  pMX (distant metastases clinically documented; not pathologically assessed in this specimen)

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A - Right Kidney, Radical Nephrectomy:
Received fresh and subsequently fixed in 10% neutral buffered formalin, labeled with the patient's name and "right kidney," is a radical nephrectomy specimen consisting of the right kidney with attached perirenal fat and Gerota's fascia. The specimen measures 18.5 x 14.0 x 10.2 cm and weighs 872 grams (unfixed). The external surface is covered by intact Gerota's fascia with no evident disruption. The perirenal adipose tissue is abundant, yellow-tan, and lobulated. The ureter is identified, measures 4.5 cm in length and 0.4 cm in diameter, and is lined by grossly unremarkable mucosa.

On sectioning, the kidney contains a large, well-circumscribed but locally invasive mass occupying the upper and mid poles, measuring 11.7 x 10.4 x 9.8 cm. The mass has a heterogeneous cut surface with predominantly yellow-golden, lobulated areas interspersed with large zones of geographic coagulative necrosis (estimated 55-60% of cut surface area), hemorrhage, and central cystic degeneration. Several areas display a firm, gray-white, whorled consistency without the typical golden-yellow appearance, comprising approximately 20% of the cut surface, morphologically consistent with the sarcomatoid component identified on prior biopsy.

The mass demonstrates macroscopic invasion into the perirenal fat, approaching but not breaching Gerota's fascia. The renal sinus fat is invaded by tumor. The main renal vein, identified at the renal hilum, is opened and contains a firm, tan-brown intraluminal tumor thrombus measuring 2.5 cm in length; the thrombus is grossly adherent to the vessel wall. The inferior vena caval margin is not identified on this specimen (intraoperatively confirmed to be free of tumor). The surgical resection margin at Gerota's fascia is grossly negative; closest margin distance is approximately 0.3 cm at the posterior aspect.

The residual non-neoplastic renal parenchyma is grossly compressed and markedly attenuated, predominantly present at the lower pole. The lower pole parenchyma appears pale tan with mild cortical thinning, consistent with chronic ischemic changes. No additional discrete masses are identified in the remaining kidney. The collecting system is patent with no calculi. No ipsilateral adrenal gland is present in the specimen.

Representative sections submitted in 24 cassettes including: tumor-perirenal fat interface (4 sections), tumor-renal sinus interface (3 sections), sarcomatoid component (3 sections), renal vein tumor thrombus (2 sections), tumor necrosis (2 sections), ureteral margin (2 sections), vascular margin at renal hilum (2 sections), residual non-neoplastic kidney (2 sections), and additional representative tumor sections (4 sections).

Specimen B - Right Para-Aortic Lymph Nodes:
Received in formalin, labeled "right para-aortic lymph nodes," is fibroadipose tissue containing 3 grossly identifiable lymph nodes measuring 3.0 cm, 1.8 cm, and 0.5 cm in greatest dimension. The largest node (3.0 cm) is sectioned and reveals tan-yellow, lobulated tissue replacing the majority of the nodal architecture with focal hemorrhage; the cut surface grossly resembles the primary renal tumor. The 1.8 cm node shows similar replacement. The 0.5 cm node is entirely submitted. All nodes submitted entirely.

Specimen C - Right Aortocaval Lymph Nodes:
Received in formalin, labeled "right aortocaval lymph nodes," is fibroadipose tissue containing 2 grossly identifiable lymph nodes measuring 1.8 cm and 0.8 cm in greatest dimension. The 1.8 cm node shows partial replacement by tan-yellow tissue. The 0.8 cm node appears grossly reactive. Both nodes submitted entirely.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A:
Histologic sections of the primary tumor confirm clear cell renal cell carcinoma. The predominant component (approximately 80% of viable tumor) consists of nests, alveolar arrays, and sheets of polygonal cells with abundant clear to lightly eosinophilic granular cytoplasm, well-defined cell borders, and delicate, branching sinusoidal vasculature — morphologically and architecturally consistent with conventional clear cell RCC. Nuclear atypia is marked, with nucleoli prominently visible at low-power magnification (10x objective) in the majority of cells, consistent with ISUP/WHO grade 4. Occasional multinucleated tumor giant cells are present. Mitotic figures are readily identifiable (average 6 per 10 high-power fields in most active areas). Geographic coagulative tumor necrosis is extensive, comprising greater than 50% of the sampled tumor area.

The sarcomatoid component (approximately 20% of viable tumor) is composed of highly pleomorphic, spindled-to-epithelioid cells arranged in fascicles and storiform patterns. These cells display marked nuclear pleomorphism, coarse chromatin, prominent nucleoli, and conspicuous mitotic activity (up to 10 per 10 high-power fields), including atypical forms. The sarcomatoid component transitions focally and abruptly from the clear cell component. Rhabdoid differentiation (cells with eccentric nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and hyaline cytoplasmic inclusions) is not identified after thorough sampling.

Tumor extension into perirenal adipose tissue and renal sinus fat is confirmed microscopically. The renal vein lumen contains adherent intraluminal tumor thrombus with direct vascular wall invasion (venous wall insinuation, not merely a floating thrombus). The Gerota's fascia is intact; the closest surgical margin is involved by perirenal fat invasion but the fascia itself is uninvolved. The ureteral margin is negative.

The non-neoplastic renal parenchyma shows moderate global glomerulosclerosis (approximately 25-30% of sampled glomeruli), interstitial fibrosis with tubular atrophy occupying approximately 20% of the cortical area, and mild arteriolar hyalinosis — changes consistent with hypertensive nephrosclerosis and/or diabetic nephropathy superimposed on surgical compression atrophy. No evidence of a concurrent second primary renal neoplasm.

Specimens B and C:
Histologic sections of lymph nodes confirm the presence of metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma. The metastatic deposits are composed of large, polygonal clear cells with prominent nucleoli arranged in nests with rich sinusoidal vasculature, morphologically identical to the primary tumor. Focal extranodal extension is identified in one of the right para-aortic nodes (Specimen B), with tumor cells extending less than 2 mm beyond the capsule into the perinodal fat. No sarcomatoid morphology is identified in the lymph node metastases.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES:

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on Specimen A, FFPE tissue blocks):

The following immunostains have been performed and are interpreted in the context of appropriate positive and negative controls:

1. PAX8: POSITIVE — diffuse nuclear staining in clear cell component; retained in sarcomatoid component (focal/weak)
2. CA-IX (Carbonic Anhydrase IX): POSITIVE — diffuse membranous staining (box-pattern) in clear cell component; focal weak in sarcomatoid component. Pattern consistent with VHL-driven HIF pathway activation.
3. Vimentin: POSITIVE — diffuse strong cytoplasmic staining in both clear cell and sarcomatoid components
4. CD10: POSITIVE — diffuse membranous/cytoplasmic staining in clear cell component
5. EMA (MUC1): POSITIVE — weak focal membranous staining in clear cell component
6. TFE3: NEGATIVE — no nuclear staining; excludes translocation-associated RCC
7. TFEB: NEGATIVE — no nuclear staining
8. CK7: NEGATIVE — absent in both clear cell and sarcomatoid components
9. CK20: NEGATIVE
10. CK AE1/AE3 (pan-cytokeratin): POSITIVE in clear cell component; NEGATIVE in sarcomatoid component (consistent with epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition in sarcomatoid areas)
11. SOX10: NEGATIVE — excludes melanocytic or nerve sheath differentiation in sarcomatoid component
12. Desmin: NEGATIVE — no evidence of myogenic differentiation in sarcomatoid component
13. BAP1 (BRCA1-Associated Protein-1): LOSS OF EXPRESSION — tumor cells (both clear cell and sarcomatoid components) show absent nuclear BAP1 staining; internal endothelial and stromal cells serve as positive internal controls. This pattern is consistent with biallelic inactivation of BAP1 (pathogenic mutation/deletion).
14. PBRM1 (BAF180): LOSS OF EXPRESSION — absent nuclear staining in tumor cells; endothelial and stromal cells show retained expression (positive internal controls). Consistent with biallelic loss-of-function of PBRM1.
15. PD-L1 (22C3 pharmDx assay, Dako): POSITIVE — Combined Positive Score (CPS) = 14. PD-L1 expression is observed in both tumor cells and tumor-infiltrating immune cells. Tumor Proportion Score (TPS) = 8%.

Note: Ki-67 proliferative index: approximately 35-40% in clear cell component; approximately 55% in sarcomatoid component (hot spots).

MOLECULAR/GENOMIC STUDIES:
Next-generation sequencing (comprehensive tumor/normal paired panel) ordered separately from this surgical specimen — results will be reported under a separate accession number once complete. Results pending at time of this report finalization.

FISH studies for specific chromosomal alterations (VHL locus, chr3p deletion) ordered on FFPE tissue from this specimen — RESULTS PENDING.

---

PATHOLOGIST'S NOTE:

The morphologic findings, in conjunction with immunohistochemical results, confirm high-grade clear cell renal cell carcinoma with sarcomatoid differentiation. The upgrade from ISUP/WHO grade 3 (as reported on core needle biopsy) to grade 4 on the nephrectomy specimen reflects sampling differences inherent to core needle biopsy versus whole-tumor examination; the most representative grading is based on the resection specimen (grade 4). The presence of sarcomatoid differentiation, BAP1 loss of expression, PBRM1 loss of expression, extensive coagulative necrosis, lymphovascular invasion (venous tumor thrombus), positive lymph nodes (3/5), and focal extranodal extension are all adverse prognostic features. These findings are consistent with the clinical staging of pT3b pN2. Results of pending molecular studies will be addended to this report.

---

ELECTRONICALLY SIGNED BY:
[Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

ADDENDUM (Pending):
FISH results for chromosome 3p deletion and VHL locus — PENDING
NGS comprehensive panel results — REPORTED UNDER SEPARATE ACCESSION

---
Report Status: FINAL (Addendum Pending)
